name: Yokohama Bay (Keawaula Beach)
tagline: The very last beach before the road ends — pristine water and almost nobody around.
parking: Free lot at Kaena Point State Park (Keawaula section); lot closes at 7pm
hours: Gate opens at sunrise, closes at 7pm
bestTime: Summer only (May–Sep); early morning for flat conditions and spinner dolphins
talkStory: Yokohama is the last sandy beach before the road ends at Kaena Point. In summer the water is crystal clear with healthy reef on the north end of the bay. This is a real wilderness beach — no food, no shops, no lifeguard. Spinner dolphins come into the bay in the mornings and monk seals occasionally haul out on the sand (stay 50 feet back, it is federal law). Strong currents can develop so stay close to shore. Combine with a hike to Kaena Point to see nesting albatross. Winter surf here is massive and deadly — summer only.
